name: perm
description: LambdaMOO-shaped permission utilities. The substrate already gives r/w/c property flags + owner, r/x/d verb flags + owner, and wizard bypass via the canBypassPerms check; everything fine-grained lives in woocode. This catalog supplies the small set of helpers that LambdaCore's $perm_utils exposes: :controls(who, what), :requires_perm(who, what, message), and the per-class :is_writable_by(who) / :is_readable_by(who) / :is_executable_by(who) convention used by $note, $block, and other catalog classes.

classes.description: Wizard-owned permission helper. Catalog code calls `"the_perm":controls(actor, target)` from verb bodies; direct calls are also allowed for diagnostics. Mirrors LambdaCore's $perm_utils:controls — "who has authority over what" — true if `who` is wizard or `who == what.owner`. Subclasses or sibling helper classes can extend with policy-specific checks (e.g. role membership), but the substrate-level bypass + owner check is the universal floor.

verbs.name: controls
verbs.source: verb :controls(who, what) rxd { if (has_flag(who, "wizard")) { return true; } if (typeof(what) != "string") { return false; } return what.owner == who; }

verbs.name: requires_perm
verbs.source: verb :requires_perm(who, what, message_text) rxd { if (this:controls(who, what)) { return true; } let msg = message_text; if (typeof(msg) != "string" || !msg) { msg = "permission denied"; } raise { code: "E_PERM", message: msg, value: { who: who, what: what } }; }

verbs.name: requires_wizard
verbs.source: verb :requires_wizard(who, message_text) rxd { if (has_flag(who, "wizard")) { return true; } let msg = message_text; if (typeof(msg) != "string" || !msg) { msg = "wizard authority required"; } raise { code: "E_PERM", message: msg, value: { who: who } }; }

seed_hooks.name: Permission helpers
seed_hooks.description: Singleton instance of $perm. Catalog code calls verbs as `"the_perm":controls(actor, target)` rather than addressing the class directly. The class itself stays a pure interface; instance lets verbs dispatch normally.
